questions.a:
Royal Journeys offers a fair tiered cancellation policy:
• 30+ days before departure: 90% refund (10% admin fee retained)
• 15–29 days before departure: 50% refund
• 7–14 days before departure: 25% refund
• Less than 7 days / no-show: No refund

All cancellation requests must be submitted in writing to bookings@royaljourneys.in

questions.q: Can I reschedule my tour instead of cancelling?
questions.a: Yes! If you reschedule at least 30 days before departure, we charge a flat ₹1,500 rescheduling fee per person. Rescheduling within 30 days is treated as a cancellation and a fresh booking.

questions.q: What if Royal Journeys cancels the tour?
questions.a: If we cancel a tour due to unforeseen circumstances (natural disaster, government advisory, insufficient group size), you get a 100% refund within 7 working days OR a free reschedule with no penalty.

questions.q: How much deposit do I need to pay to confirm a booking?
questions.a: A 25% non-refundable deposit is required to confirm your booking. The remaining 75% balance is due 30 days before departure.

questions.q: What payment methods do you accept?
questions.a:
We accept:
• NEFT / IMPS bank transfer
• UPI (PhonePe, GPay, Paytm) – ID: royaljourneys@ybl
• Credit/debit card via our secure payment link
• Cheque for full payment (30+ days before departure only)
Yes, EMI is available on credit cards (3/6/9 months) through our payment gateway. A processing fee of 2% applies for EMI options.

questions.q: Will I get a receipt / invoice?
questions.a: Yes. A GST invoice is issued within 24 hours of receiving your payment. All bookings come with a booking confirmation document listing all inclusions.

No specific vaccinations are required for domestic India tours. However, we recommend being up to date on routine vaccinations. For Andaman trips, anti-malarial precautions are advised for some remote areas.
Travel insurance is strongly recommended but not mandatory. It covers trip cancellation, medical emergencies, and lost baggage. We can arrange a policy for approximately ₹500–800 per person per trip.

questions.q: What if I fall sick during the tour?
questions.a: Our tour escort will assist you in finding medical help immediately. If you have travel insurance, most expenses are covered. We have tie-ups with clinics in all our tour destinations.

questions.q: Are your tours suitable for senior citizens?
questions.a: Most of our tours are suitable for seniors aged 60–75 with moderate fitness. For Manali (high altitude) and adventure-heavy tours, we recommend a doctor's clearance. Goa, Kerala, and Rajasthan are very senior-friendly.

questions.q: What documents do I need to carry?
questions.a:
• Original government-issued photo ID (Aadhar, Passport, Voter ID, or Driving License)
• Booking confirmation email from Royal Journeys
• Airline / train tickets
• Travel insurance policy document (if purchased)
• Passport-size photos (2 copies) – needed for some monument permits

questions.q: Is a passport required for domestic tours?
questions.a: A passport is not required for domestic India tours. A valid Aadhar card or Voter ID is sufficient.

questions.q: Do I need a permit for Andaman?
questions.a: Indian nationals do not need a permit for Port Blair, Havelock, and Neil Island. A Restricted Area Permit (RAP) is required only for outer tribal islands, which we arrange if your itinerary includes them.

questions.q: Can you arrange custom tours for large groups?
questions.a: Absolutely! Royal Journeys specialises in corporate outings, school trips, family reunions, and wedding groups. For groups of 10+, we offer custom itineraries, dedicated tour managers, and special group pricing. Contact us for a tailored quote.

questions.q: Is there a discount for group bookings?
questions.a:
Yes:
• 5–9 pax: 5% group discount
• 10–19 pax: 10% group discount
• 20+ pax: 15% group discount + complimentary tour manager

Group discounts cannot be combined with other promotions.
